title: Implement AppExchange Global OAuth for Cross-Org Salesforce Authentication
description:
Salesforce deprecated the traditional Connected App creation flow in newer orgs, replacing it with External Client Apps that are org-scoped (one app per org). This prevents a single OAuth app from working across all customer orgs.

### Problem
Without a cross-org OAuth solution, each customer would need to create and manage their own Salesforce OAuth app, which is not scalable.

### Solution: AppExchange Global OAuth
- Create a private AppExchange listing for DataTools
- Configure Global OAuth during AppExchange setup
- Use the issued Global Consumer Key and Secret for all Salesforce OAuth flows
- Maintain a single OAuth callback URL shared across all customers

### Scope
1. Create AppExchange partner account
2. Set up private AppExchange listing
3. Configure Global OAuth (Authorization Code Flow with PKCE, single callback URL, required OAuth scopes)
4. Update DataTools OAuth implementation to use Global OAuth credentials
5. Prepare and submit Salesforce Security Review documentation
6. Address Salesforce review feedback
acceptance_criteria: A single Salesforce OAuth client is used across all customer orgs
Any Salesforce org can authenticate without creating a local OAuth app
OAuth flow works for both production and sandbox orgs
Customer onboarding does not require Salesforce admin OAuth configuration
middleware_changes: Replace per-org Connected App OAuth with AppExchange Global OAuth credentials. Update OAuth flow to use Authorization Code Flow with PKCE.
api_changes: Updated Salesforce OAuth integration to use Global OAuth Consumer Key and Secret from AppExchange listing.
implementation_notes: This is the Salesforce-supported approach for multi-tenant SaaS platforms (used by HubSpot, Tableau, Fivetran). Requires AppExchange partner account and security review. External Client Apps are org-scoped and cannot replace Connected Apps for cross-org use.
